title: Bungie lays off most of the Destiny 2 team and part of the Marathon team
summary: Sony and Bungie confirm a restructuring that leads to the layoff of most Destiny 2 developers, while the studio focuses on Marathon.

title: GTA 6 sparks controversy over its price, lack of disc, and pre-order requirements
summary: Grand Theft Auto VI captures attention with announcements about its editions, prices, and the decision not to include a physical disc, generating debate among players.

title: Valve clarifies the future of Steam Machine amid criticism and expectations
summary: The company responds to criticism about the console's performance and assures that it will receive updates similar to Steam Deck, in addition to proposing new models.

title: Microsoft raises Xbox Series prices worldwide due to component crisis
summary: The company increases the cost of all Xbox Series consoles, discontinues the 2 TB model, and offers loan programs to mitigate the impact.

title: Deltarune Chapter 5 launches with great success, announces chapter 6 for 2027
summary: The new chapter of Toby Fox's indie RPG reaches nearly 300,000 concurrent players on Steam and is already preparing its continuation.

title: Steam Summer Sale 2026 begins with big discounts
summary: Valve starts its traditional sales event with thousands of games on sale, including PC Gamer's GOTY for $24 and other notable titles.

title: Xbox prepares for wave of layoffs as Compulsion Games employees announce departure
summary: Amid reports of upcoming job cuts at Xbox, several developers from the South of Midnight studio confirm they are seeking new opportunities.

title: Component crisis drives up RAM prices and threatens tech industry
summary: The unprecedented increase in memory and storage costs causes price hikes in PCs, consoles, and Apple devices, and is expected to continue.

title: Wildgate ends development one year after launch
summary: The space shooter from former Blizzard developers receives its final update and ceases active development, although the game is not shutting down.

title: Capcom shows first gameplay of Onimusha: Way of the Sword
summary: The Japanese company releases a new trailer and screenshots offering a first look at the action and sword game.

title: Dragon's Dogma 2: Dark Arisen reveals new features and gameplay
summary: Capcom presents a new video with details of the highly anticipated DLC for the open-world RPG.

title: Enshrouded announces final release date for October 2026
summary: The survival and action game will arrive on PC and PS5 in October, and on Xbox Series in spring 2027.

title: Marvel's Wolverine will have physical disc edition, unlike GTA 6
summary: Insomniac Games confirms that the Wolverine game for PS5 will include a disc in the box, contrasting with the digital trend of other releases.

title: Monster Hunter Stories 3 receives new story DLC and free update
summary: Capcom releases the 'Rudy' additional content along with an update that adds real monsters to the RPG.

title: Sonic ARG sparks controversy by asking for consent to use data for AI
summary: Players criticize SEGA for an alternate reality game that requests permission to train artificial intelligence with user information.

title: FromSoftware parent company CEO retains position despite investor criticism
summary: Activist investors expressed discontent over Elden Ring management, but the board of directors decides to keep its top executive.

title: Riot Games modifies Vanguard to run only during matches
summary: The company updates its anti-cheat system to be less intrusive and only activate when starting League of Legends, responding to community complaints.

title: Supergirl receives divided opinions on its cinematic debut
summary: The DC Universe film is now in theaters, generating debate about its quality while confirming it has a post-credit scene.

title: The Drifter, the dark horror game that triumphs on Switch 2
summary: The indie graphic adventure title receives excellent reviews and is compared to classics from Sierra and LucasArts.

title: Epic Games slams Valve's AI policy on Steam as irresponsible
summary: Tim Sweeney criticizes Steam's decision to require developers to disclose the use of artificial intelligence in their games.
